title: The Sunken Vault — Entrance
description: A stone threshold, old enough that the mason's marks on its lintel have been worn to shallow grooves by centuries of wind and creeping moss. The sealing stone that once blocked the entrance has been levered aside and left canted against the wall — someone pried it open weeks ago and never replaced it. The smell that rises from below is ancient and cold, the particular rot of a place that has been closed off from the living world for a very long time and has just been reminded that the world exists.

exits.description: an archway leading deeper into the vault
